skill.name: commit-chat-push
skill.description: Commit and push repository changes while also exporting and committing the Codex chat/session transcript that produced the feature. Use when the user asks Codex to commit, push, save provenance, include the chat, commit the session, attach the Codex conversation, or preserve the implementation chat from ~/.codex/sessions alongside code changes.
skill.summary: Commit code changes with an exported Codex transcript for full implementation provenance
skill.author: bertona88

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate git workflow tool for committing code with Codex session provenance. Static findings are largely false positives: SHA256 is used for hashing file digests (not weak crypto for security), backtick patterns are documentation artifacts, and the CRITICAL blockers on ransomware/recursive delete refer to git rm commands used appropriately for git operations. The skill includes a built-in redaction system and explicit workflow steps to prevent secret leakage.

high_findings.title: Command Execution in Documentation
high_findings.description: Documentation files contain examples of shell command patterns including backticks, command substitution, and Unix shell invocations. These are documented examples, not executable code in the skill itself.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detection flags shell patterns in documentation markdown - these are legitimate examples of git operations, not runtime code execution threats

medium_findings.title: Redaction System Detected
medium_findings.description: The skill includes a redaction system to remove secrets from exported transcripts. The system uses regex patterns to detect and redact API keys, tokens, and sensitive credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Redaction is a deliberate security feature implemented in the skill to protect users from accidentally committing secrets - this is a positive finding, not a vulnerability

medium_findings.title: Home Directory Path Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill accesses ~/.codex/sessions for transcript files and shortens home paths to ~ in output for privacy. This is expected behavior for a Codex session export tool.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Accessing Codex session files from the standard home directory location is required functionality for this skill

low_findings.title: Cryptographic Hash Usage
low_findings.description: SHA256 is used for file digest computation to track transcript source files. This is appropriate cryptographic usage for integrity verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SHA256 is used for non-security hashing of file contents to create a digest for verification purposes - this is appropriate and secure usage

low_findings.title: File System Access for Transcript Export
low_findings.description: The skill creates and writes transcript files to the repository. This is the intended purpose of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Creating output files in the docs/codex-sessions directory is the core functionality of this skill

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analysis False Positives in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The static scanner flagged CRITICAL patterns (ransomware keywords, recursive delete) in documentation markdown files that describe git operations. These are legitimate git commands for removing tracked files, not malicious patterns.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Scanner flagged 'rm -rf' in documentation context for git cleanup workflows - legitimate documentation of git rm commands, not actual destructive operations

content.user_title: Commit code with Codex session transcript
content.value_statement: Developers want to track AI-assisted implementation history but risk exposing secrets. This skill exports redacted Codex session transcripts and commits them alongside code changes with full git workflow safety checks.
actual_capabilities: Export redacted Markdown transcripts from Codex JSONL session files
Redact API keys, tokens, passwords, and private keys from transcripts
Commit code changes with transcript alongside in a single workflow
Match sessions to repositories by working directory
Scan transcripts for secrets before committing
limitations: Does not modify or delete code, only commits existing changes
Cannot push to repositories without git remote configuration
Transcript redaction is regex-based and may miss novel secret formats
Requires Codex session JSONL files to be present in ~/.codex/sessions

use_cases.title: Track AI implementation decisions
use_cases.description: Commit features with full conversation history so reviewers can understand why specific approaches were chosen without re-running sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers who need to document AI-assisted implementation choices for team review

use_cases.title: Create auditable development records
use_cases.description: Generate source-controlled provenance records for compliance, debugging, or knowledge transfer when AI tools contributed to the codebase.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams requiring implementation audit trails for regulatory or quality assurance purposes

use_cases.title: Review AI pair programming sessions
use_cases.description: Export and examine past Codex conversations to understand implementation context, verify decisions, or learn from AI suggestions.
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers who want to review or learn from their AI pair programming sessions

prompt_templates.title: Basic commit with transcript
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $commit-chat-push to commit these changes, add the Codex chat transcript, and push the branch.
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard workflow when you have finished implementing a feature and want to save both the code and the AI conversation

prompt_templates.title: Commit without pushing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $commit-chat-push to commit these changes with the transcript. Do not push yet.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to review the commit locally before pushing to remote

prompt_templates.title: Export specific session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $commit-chat-push to export the session from this afternoon, commit the transcript, and push.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to commit a transcript from a specific Codex session rather than the most recent one

prompt_templates.title: Verify before commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $commit-chat-push to commit these changes and include the transcript. Scan for secrets first and show me the results before staging.
prompt_templates.scenario: When security review of the transcript is required before committing

output_examples.input: Use $commit-chat-push to commit these changes and push the branch.
output_examples.output: I will commit your changes and export the Codex session transcript. Let me start by checking the repository state and finding the relevant session.

output_examples.input: Commit with transcript but skip pushing
output_examples.output: I will commit your changes and the transcript file. I will not push until you confirm.

output_examples.input: Show me the transcript before committing
output_examples.output: I found the Codex session. Here is a preview of the transcript that will be committed. Please review and confirm if you would like to proceed with the commit.

best_practices: Review the exported transcript for sensitive content before confirming the commit
Use explicit file staging rather than git add . when the worktree contains unrelated changes
Configure your repository to use conventional commit messages for consistent history
anti_patterns: Do not use raw JSONL sessions when the transcript includes system instructions or developer messages
Avoid committing transcripts that contain API keys or tokens even after redaction
Do not skip the git diff review step when making large changes

faq.question: Does this skill read my private conversations?
faq.answer: The skill reads Codex session JSONL files from your local ~/.codex/sessions directory to create a redacted transcript. It does not send data anywhere.

faq.question: How does the redaction work?
faq.answer: The exporter uses regex patterns to detect common secret formats like API keys, GitHub tokens, and private keys, replacing them with [REDACTED] in the output.

faq.question: Can I review the transcript before committing?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill workflow includes a step to review the exported transcript and scan for obvious secrets before staging.

faq.question: What happens if the session contains sensitive information?
faq.answer: You can rerun the exporter with stricter omission options, manually edit the transcript, or skip committing the transcript file entirely.

faq.question: Where are transcripts stored?
faq.answer: Transcripts are written to docs/codex-sessions/ by default, or to your repository convention if it uses a different folder name.

faq.question: Can I use this without Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The exporter script can be run directly on any Codex JSONL session file without using the skill invocation.
